Don't you feel well?","notes":"Present tense with reflexive pronoun 'sich'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ehrlich gesagt, mir ist seit gestern übel und ich habe Kopfschmerzen. Vielleicht sollte ich zum Arzt gehen.","translation":"To be honest, I've been nauseous since yesterday and I have headaches. Maybe I should go to the doctor.","notes":"Modal verb 'sollte' (should) + infinitive"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das ist eine gute Idee. Hast du schon einen Termin vereinbart oder wartest du noch?","translation":"That's a good idea. Have you already made an appointment or are you still waiting?","notes":"Perfect tense with 'haben' + past participle"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich habe vorhin angerufen, aber die Sprechstunden sind heute leider schon ausgebucht.","translation":"I called earlier, but unfortunately the office hours are already fully booked today.","notes":"Perfect tense and passive construction with 'sind ausgebucht'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Dann könntest du zur Notaufnahme im Krankenhaus fahren, wenn es wirklich schlimm ist.","translation":"Then you could go to the emergency room at the hospital if it's really bad.","notes":"Conditional with 'könntest' + infinitive"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Meinst du, das ist nötig? Ich möchte nicht übertreiben, aber die Beschwerden werden immer stärker.","translation":"Do you think that's necessary? I don't want to exaggerate, but the symptoms are getting stronger.","notes":"Comparative form 'stärker' and present continuous aspect"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Besser, du lässt dich untersuchen, als dass du es bereust. Gesundheit geht vor allem anderen.","translation":"Better to get examined than to regret it. Health comes before everything else.","notes":"Subjunctive construction with 'als dass' and separable verb 'vorgehen'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Du hast recht. Ich werde gleich nach der Arbeit zur Praxis fahren und nachfragen, ob sie mich dazwischenschieben können.","translation":"You're right. I'll go to the practice right after work and ask if they can squeeze me in.","notes":"Future tense with 'werden' + infinitive and separable verb"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Vergiss nicht, deine Krankenversicherungskarte mitzunehmen! Ohne die geht gar nichts.","translation":"Don't forget to take your health insurance card! Nothing works without it.","notes":"Imperative form and separable verb 'mitnehmen'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Stimmt, die liegt noch zu Hause in meiner Geldbörse. Ich werde sie gleich holen, wenn ich Feierabend habe.","translation":"Right, it's still at home in my wallet. I'll get it right away when I finish work.","notes":"Future tense and temporal clause with 'wenn'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Weißt du eigentlich, welcher Arzt für deine Beschwerden zuständig ist? Ein Hausarzt oder ein Spezialist?","translation":"Do you actually know which doctor is responsible for your symptoms? A general practitioner or a specialist?","notes":"Question with 'welcher' (which) and relative clause"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich denke, zuerst gehe ich zum Hausarzt. Der kann mich dann bei Bedarf an einen Facharzt überweisen.","translation":"I think I'll go to the general practitioner first. He can then refer me to a specialist if needed.","notes":"Modal verb construction and separable verb 'überweisen'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das klingt vernünftig. Hast du dir schon Gedanken über mögliche Ursachen gemacht?","translation":"That sounds reasonable. Have you already thought about possible causes?","notes":"Perfect tense with reflexive construction 'sich Gedanken machen'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Vielleicht liegt es am Stress bei der Arbeit. In letzter Zeit arbeite ich ziemlich viel Überstunden.","translation":"Maybe it's due to stress at work. Lately, I've been working quite a lot of overtime.","notes":"Causal construction with 'liegt an' and temporal expression"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das könnte durchaus der Grund sein. Stress kann zu verschiedenen körperlichen Problemen führen.","translation":"That could very well be the reason. Stress can lead to various physical problems.","notes":"Modal verb 'könnte' and preposition 'zu' with infinitive construction"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, aber ich will trotzdem auf Nummer sicher gehen und mich gründlich untersuchen lassen.","translation":"Yes, but I still want to be on the safe side and get thoroughly examined.","notes":"Idiomatic expression 'auf Nummer sicher gehen' and reflexive construction"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das ist die richtige Einstellung. Früherkennung ist bei gesundheitlichen Problemen sehr wichtig.","translation":"That's the right attitude. Early detection is very important with health problems.","notes":"Adjective declension and compound noun 'Früherkennung'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich hoffe nur, dass die Untersuchung nicht zu unangenehm wird. Ich bin ehrlich gesagt etwas nervös.","translation":"I just hope the examination won't be too unpleasant. I'm honestly a bit nervous.","notes":"Subjunctive construction with 'dass' and adverbial use of 'ehrlich gesagt'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das kann ich verstehen, aber die meisten Ärzte sind sehr einfühlsam und erklären jeden Schritt.","translation":"I can understand that, but most doctors are very empathetic and explain every step.","notes":"Modal verb 'können' and superlative 'die meisten'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Falls der Arzt Blut abnehmen muss, werde ich wahrscheinlich wegschauen. Das macht mich immer schwindelig.","translation":"If the doctor has to take blood, I'll probably look away. That always makes me dizzy.","notes":"Conditional clause with 'falls' and separable verb 'wegschauen'"}],"challenges":[]},{"id":1777210517007,"title":"Urlaubszeit: Abenteuer und Entspannung","topic":"✈️ Holiday","level":"B1","standard":"goethe","module":"sprechen","durLabel":"Standard","vocab":[{"word":"Urlaub","article":"der","baseForm":"Urlaub","translation":"vacation","synonyms":["Ferien"],"partOfSpeech":"noun","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Ich mache im Sommer Urlaub.","exampleEn":"I'm going on vacation in the summer.","podcastContext":"holiday","module":"sprechen"},{"word":"Reise","article":"die","baseForm":"Reise","translation":"trip","synonyms":["Tour"],"partOfSpeech":"noun","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Die Reise nach Italien war wunderbar.","exampleEn":"The trip to Italy was wonderful.","podcastContext":"holiday","module":"sprechen"},{"word":"Strand","article":"der","baseForm":"Strand","translation":"beach","synonyms":["Küste"],"partOfSpeech":"noun","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Wir verbringen den Tag am Strand.","exampleEn":"We spend the day at the beach.","podcastContext":"holiday","module":"sprechen"},{"word":"Abenteuer","article":"das","baseForm":"Abenteuer","translation":"adventure","synonyms":["Erlebnis"],"partOfSpeech":"noun","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Ein Urlaub in der Wildnis ist ein Abenteuer.","exampleEn":"A vacation in the wilderness is an adventure.","podcastContext":"holiday","module":"sprechen"}],"createdAt":"2026-04-26T13:35:17.007Z","bestScore":0,"dialogue":[{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Max, hast du schon Pläne für deinen nächsten Urlaub? Ich freue mich immer so auf die Ferien.","translation":"Max, do you already have plans for your next vacation? I always look forward to the holidays.","notes":"Use 'schon' to ask if something is already planned or done."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, ich plane eine Reise nach Spanien. Die Strände dort sind einfach fantastisch!","translation":"Yes, I'm planning a trip to Spain. The beaches there are simply fantastic!","notes":"Use 'nach' with country names when talking about traveling to them."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das klingt toll! Warst du schon einmal in Spanien?","translation":"That sounds great! Have you been to Spain before?","notes":"The use of 'schon einmal' is common to ask if someone has ever done something."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, letztes Jahr war ich in Barcelona. Die Stadt ist voller Leben und Kultur.","translation":"Yes, last year I was in Barcelona. The city is full of life and culture.","notes":"Use 'letztes Jahr' for past events to refer specifically to the last year."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ah, Barcelona ist wirklich schön. Ich war dort vor zwei Jahren.","translation":"Ah, Barcelona is really beautiful. I was there two years ago.","notes":"Use 'vor' with time expressions to indicate how long ago something happened."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Was hast du dort gemacht? Hast du die Sagrada Familia besucht?","translation":"What did you do there? Did you visit the Sagrada Familia?","notes":"Use 'hast du' to form yes/no questions in the past tense."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, ich habe die Sagrada Familia gesehen. Sie war beeindruckend!","translation":"Yes, I saw the Sagrada Familia. It was impressive!","notes":"Use 'gesehen' as the past participle of 'sehen' in perfect tense."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Und welches Essen hat dir am besten geschmeckt?","translation":"And which food did you like best?","notes":"Use 'am besten' to indicate the superlative form of 'good' or 'tasty'."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich habe Tapas geliebt! Sie sind so vielfältig und lecker.","translation":"I loved tapas! They are so diverse and delicious.","notes":"Use 'geliebt' as the past participle of 'lieben'."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Stimmt, Tapas sind wirklich köstlich. Welche Orte möchtest du in Zukunft noch besuchen?","translation":"True, tapas are really delicious. Which places would you still like to visit in the future?","notes":"Use 'möchtest' for polite forms of expressing desires."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich möchte gerne nach Japan reisen. Die Kultur dort fasziniert mich.","translation":"I would like to travel to Japan. The culture there fascinates me.","notes":"Use 'möchte' to express a wish or desire."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Japan klingt aufregend! Hast du schon eine Reiseroute im Kopf?","translation":"Japan sounds exciting! Do you already have a travel itinerary in mind?","notes":"Use 'im Kopf' to express having something in mind."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Noch nicht genau, aber Tokio und Kyoto stehen auf meiner Liste.","translation":"Not exactly yet, but Tokyo and Kyoto are on my list.","notes":"Use 'stehen auf meiner Liste' for things that are planned or intended."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das sind bestimmt beeindruckende Städte. Ich träume auch von einer Reise nach Asien.","translation":"Those are certainly impressive cities. I also dream of a trip to Asia.","notes":"Use 'träume von' to express dreaming or longing for something."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Vielleicht könnten wir eines Tages zusammen reisen. Hast du schon Urlaubstage für dieses Jahr geplant?","translation":"Maybe we could travel together one day. Have you already planned vacation days for this year?","notes":"Use 'könnten' for hypothetical or potential actions."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, ich habe im Sommer zwei Wochen frei. Ich überlege, wohin ich reisen soll.","translation":"Yes, I have two weeks off in the summer. I'm considering where I should travel to.","notes":"Use 'überlege' when thinking about or considering options."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Wie wäre es mit Italien? Die Landschaft und das Essen sind herrlich.","translation":"How about Italy? The scenery and the food are wonderful.","notes":"Use 'wie wäre es mit' to suggest an idea or option."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das ist eine gute Idee. Ich war noch nie in Italien.","translation":"That's a good idea. I've never been to Italy.","notes":"Use 'noch nie' to indicate that something has never been experienced."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Du wirst es lieben! Besonders die Toskana ist sehr malerisch.","translation":"You will love it! Especially Tuscany is very picturesque.","notes":"Use 'besonders' to emphasize a particular detail."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich werde darüber nachdenken. Vielleicht buche ich bald meine Flüge.","translation":"I will think about it. Maybe I'll book my flights soon.","notes":"Use 'vielleicht' for expressing uncertainty or possibility."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Gute Idee. Es ist immer gut, früh zu buchen, um Geld zu sparen.","translation":"Good idea. It's always good to book early to save money.","notes":"Use 'um zu' for expressing purpose or intent."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Hast du schon einmal einen Last-Minute-Urlaub gemacht?","translation":"Have you ever taken a last-minute vacation?","notes":"Use 'schon einmal' when asking if someone has ever done something."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, letztes Jahr. Es war spontan, aber sehr spannend.","translation":"Yes, last year. It was spontaneous but very exciting.","notes":"Use 'spontan' for actions done without prior planning."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das klingt abenteuerlich. War das nicht stressig?","translation":"That sounds adventurous. Wasn't that stressful?","notes":"Use 'war das nicht' for rhetorical or confirming questions."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ein bisschen, aber der Nervenkitzel war es wert!","translation":"A little bit, but the thrill was worth it!","notes":"Use 'es wert' to express that something is worth the effort or risk."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Vielleicht probiere ich das auch mal aus. Welche Tipps hast du für spontane Reisen?","translation":"Maybe I'll try that too. What tips do you have for spontaneous trips?","notes":"Use 'probieren' for trying out or testing something."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Sei flexibel und offen für Neues. Und vergiss nicht, die wichtigsten Dinge zu packen.","translation":"Be flexible and open to new things. And don't forget to pack the essentials.","notes":"Use 'die wichtigsten Dinge' to refer to essentials or necessities."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das sind gute Ratschläge. Ich werde sie beherzigen.","translation":"Those are good pieces of advice. I will keep them in mind.","notes":"Use 'beherzigen' to express taking advice to heart or following it."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich bin sicher, du wirst es nicht bereuen. Und jetzt noch eine Frage: Strand oder Berge?","translation":"I'm sure you won't regret it. And now one more question: beach or mountains?","notes":"Use 'wirst es nicht bereuen' to reassure someone that they won't regret something."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Definitiv Strand! Ich liebe das Meer und die Sonne.","translation":"Definitely beach! I love the sea and the sun.","notes":"Use 'definitiv' for emphasizing a strong personal preference."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich mag beides, aber die Berge haben etwas Magisches.","translation":"I like both, but the mountains have something magical.","notes":"Use 'haben etwas Magisches' to express an enchanting or special quality."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Vielleicht mache ich mal eine Bergwanderung. Das könnte eine neue Erfahrung sein.","translation":"Maybe I'll go hiking in the mountains. That could be a new experience.","notes":"Use 'könnte' for possibilities that are not certain yet."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das solltest du wirklich ausprobieren. Es ist gut für Körper und Geist.","translation":"You should really try that. It's good for body and mind.","notes":"Use 'solltest' for giving advice or suggestions."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Danke für den Tipp, Anna. Hast du noch andere Reiseziele auf deiner Wunschliste?","translation":"Thanks for the tip, Anna. Do you have any other travel destinations on your wish list?","notes":"Use 'Wunschliste' to refer to a list of desires or goals."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, Australien steht ganz oben. Die Natur dort ist faszinierend.","translation":"Yes, Australia is at the top. The nature there is fascinating.","notes":"Use 'steht ganz oben' to indicate a top priority or interest."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Australien ist bestimmt spannend. Hast du schon Pläne, wann du dorthin reisen möchtest?","translation":"Australia is definitely exciting. Do you already have plans for when you want to travel there?","notes":"Use 'wann' for questions about time or dates."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Noch nicht, aber vielleicht nächstes Jahr. Es wäre ein Abenteuer.","translation":"Not yet, but maybe next year. It would be an adventure.","notes":"Use 'nächstes Jahr' to talk about plans for the upcoming year."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich wünsche dir viel Spaß bei deinen zukünftigen Reisen. Lass uns bald wieder darüber sprechen!","translation":"I wish you lots of fun on your future trips. Let's talk about it again soon!","notes":"Use 'viel Spaß' to express good wishes for enjoyment and fun."}],"challenges":[{"situation":"You are planning a trip and want to know about the weather.","cue":"How to ask about the weather forecast?","cueHint":"Wettervorhersage","targetAnswer":"Wie ist die Wettervorhersage für nächste Woche?","difficulty":"easy"},{"situation":"You want to suggest a trip to the mountains.","cue":"What to suggest?","cueHint":"Berge","targetAnswer":"Wie wäre es mit einem Ausflug in die Berge?","difficulty":"medium"},{"situation":"Your friend mentions a spontaneous trip.","cue":"How to express interest?","cueHint":"interessiert","targetAnswer":"Das klingt interessant! Erzähl mir mehr.","difficulty":"easy"}]},{"id":1777209646533,"title":"Am Bahnhof - Eine Reise mit Anna und Max","topic":"🚂 Train Station","level":"B1","standard":"telc","module":"sprechen","durLabel":"Basic","vocab":[{"word":"der Bahnhof","article":"der","baseForm":"Bahnhof","translation":"train station","synonyms":["die Station"],"partOfSpeech":"noun","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Der Bahnhof ist sehr groß.","exampleEn":"The train station is very large.","podcastContext":"travel","module":"reisen"},{"word":"reisen","article":"n/a","baseForm":"reisen","translation":"to travel","synonyms":["fahren"],"partOfSpeech":"verb","level":"B1","exampleDe":"Ich reise gerne mit dem Zug.","exampleEn":"I like traveling by train.","podcastContext":"travel","module":"sprechen"}],"createdAt":"2026-04-26T13:20:46.533Z","bestScore":0,"dialogue":[{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Hallo Max, hast du schon die neuen Züge am Bahnhof gesehen? Sie sollen sehr modern und schnell sein.","translation":"Hello Max, have you seen the new trains at the station? They are supposed to be very modern and fast.","notes":"The verb 'sehen' is used here in the perfect tense, which is commonly used in spoken German."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, Anna, ich habe sie gesehen. Sie sind wirklich beeindruckend! Ich liebe es, mit dem Zug zu reisen.","translation":"Yes, Anna, I've seen them. They are really impressive! I love traveling by train.","notes":"The phrase 'mit dem Zug' uses the dative case after 'mit', a common preposition."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich auch! Es ist so entspannend, aus dem Fenster zu schauen und die Landschaft zu genießen.","translation":"Me too! It's so relaxing to look out the window and enjoy the landscape.","notes":"The infinitive 'zu' is used here with 'genießen', showing an action to be performed."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Stimmt, und man kann dabei lesen oder Musik hören. Was machst du normalerweise während einer Zugfahrt?","translation":"True, and you can read or listen to music. What do you usually do during a train ride?","notes":"'während' is a preposition that requires the genitive case, but in spoken German, the dative is often used."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Meistens lese ich ein Buch oder plane meine Reise. Hast du ein Lieblingsbuch, das du im Zug liest?","translation":"Most of the time, I read a book or plan my trip. Do you have a favorite book that you read on the train?","notes":"'das Lieblingsbuch' combines 'Lieblings-' with a noun, a common way to form compound nouns."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, ich lese gerne Krimis, besonders die von Agatha Christie. Sie sind spannend und perfekt für unterwegs.","translation":"Yes, I like to read crime novels, especially those by Agatha Christie. They are exciting and perfect for traveling.","notes":"'besonders' is an adverb used to emphasize a preference."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich mag auch Krimis, aber ich bevorzuge deutsche Autoren. Kennst du Sebastian Fitzek?","translation":"I also like crime novels, but I prefer German authors. Do you know Sebastian Fitzek?","notes":"'bevorzugen' is a verb used to express preference."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, natürlich kenne ich ihn! Seine Bücher sind wirklich fesselnd. Welches Buch von ihm magst du am liebsten?","translation":"Yes, of course I know him! His books are really captivating. Which book of his do you like the most?","notes":"'fesselnd' is an adjective used to describe something engaging or captivating."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ich finde 'Passagier 23' besonders gut. Die Geschichte ist voller überraschender Wendungen.","translation":"I find 'Passenger 23' particularly good. The story is full of surprising twists.","notes":"'voller' is used with the genitive to indicate 'full of' something."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das klingt interessant. Ich werde es mir beim nächsten Mal ausleihen. Fährst du oft mit der Bahn, Anna?","translation":"That sounds interesting. I'll borrow it next time. Do you often travel by train, Anna?","notes":"'ausleihen' means to borrow and can be used in the context of borrowing books."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, ziemlich oft. Es ist praktisch, vor allem auf langen Strecken. Wo fährst du am liebsten mit dem Zug hin?","translation":"Yes, quite often. It's practical, especially on long distances. Where do you most like to go by train?","notes":"'vor allem' is an adverbial phrase meaning 'especially'."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich fahre gerne nach München. Die Zugfahrt durch Bayern ist wunderschön, besonders im Herbst.","translation":"I like traveling to Munich. The train ride through Bavaria is beautiful, especially in autumn.","notes":"'besonders' again emphasizes a particular preference or time."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Oh ja, die Landschaft dort ist herrlich. Ich war letzten Herbst in München und es war großartig.","translation":"Oh yes, the landscape there is wonderful. I was in Munich last autumn and it was great.","notes":"'letzten Herbst' is an example of using the accusative to indicate time."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Hast du den Englischen Garten besucht? Er ist einer meiner Lieblingsorte in München.","translation":"Did you visit the English Garden? It's one of my favorite places in Munich.","notes":"'einer meiner Lieblingsorte' is a genitive construction indicating possession or relationship."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, ich war dort. Es war so friedlich und schön. Ich habe dort ein Picknick gemacht.","translation":"Yes, I was there. It was so peaceful and beautiful. I had a picnic there.","notes":"The phrase 'ein Picknick machen' is a common way to say 'to have a picnic'."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Das klingt wunderbar. Vielleicht sollten wir einmal zusammen dorthin fahren.","translation":"That sounds wonderful. Maybe we should go there together sometime.","notes":"'vielleicht' is an adverb used to suggest a possibility."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Gute Idee, Max! Wir könnten auch die Museen besuchen. Ich liebe Kunstmuseen.","translation":"Good idea, Max! We could also visit the museums. I love art museums.","notes":"'könnten' is the subjunctive form of 'können', used here to suggest a possibility."},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ich auch, besonders moderne Kunst. Weißt du, ob es in München gute Ausstellungen gibt?","translation":"Me too, especially modern art. Do you know if there are good exhibitions in Munich?","notes":"The use of 'ob' introduces an indirect question."},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, ich habe gehört, dass die Pinakotheken großartige Ausstellungen haben. Wir sollten das unbedingt machen.","translation":"Yes, I've heard that the Pinakotheken have great exhibitions. store.sp3_story: [{"title":"Mit dem Mietwagen auf die Autobahn","scenario":"Renting a car and navigating the Autobahn","level":"B1","dialogue":[{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Max, wir müssen heute unbedingt ein Auto mieten. Meine Schwester wohnt in München, und mit dem Zug dauert es vier Stunden.","translation":"Max, we absolutely have to rent a car today. My sister lives in Munich, and it takes four hours by train.","notes":"modal verb 'müssen' with infinitive, compound adverb 'unbedingt'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Gute Idee! Ich habe schon online nach Mietwagen gesucht. Bei Sixt gibt es ein Angebot für ein kompaktes Fahrzeug - nur 45 Euro pro Tag.","translation":"Good idea! I've already searched for rental cars online. At Sixt there's an offer for a compact vehicle - only 45 euros per day.","notes":"Perfekt tense with 'haben', preposition 'bei' with dative"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Guten Tag! Sie möchten also einen Wagen mieten? Haben Sie beide einen gültigen Führerschein dabei?","translation":"Good day! So you'd like to rent a car? Do you both have a valid driver's license with you?","notes":"polite 'Sie' form, adjective 'gültig' with dative"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Ja, hier sind unsere Führerscheine. Ich habe meinen deutschen vor zwei Jahren gemacht, und Max hat einen internationalen.","translation":"Yes, here are our driver's licenses. I got my German one two years ago, and Max has an international one.","notes":"temporal expression with 'vor', possessive pronouns"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Perfekt. Welche Kategorie bevorzugen Sie? Wir haben Kleinwagen, Kompaktklasse oder auch größere Fahrzeuge verfügbar.","translation":"Perfect. Which category do you prefer? We have small cars, compact class or also larger vehicles available.","notes":"question word 'welche', adjective 'verfügbar' as predicate"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ein Kompaktwagen wäre ideal. Wir fahren auf die Autobahn nach München, deshalb brauchen wir etwas mit genügend Power.","translation":"A compact car would be ideal. We're driving on the Autobahn to Munich, so we need something with enough power.","notes":"Konjunktiv II 'wäre', causal conjunction 'deshalb'"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Dann empfehle ich Ihnen diesen VW Golf. Er hat einen sparsamen Motor und fährt sich sehr angenehm auf längeren Strecken.","translation":"Then I recommend this VW Golf to you. It has an economical engine and drives very pleasantly on longer routes.","notes":"dative object with 'empfehlen', reflexive verb 'sich fahren'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Das klingt gut. Ist eine Vollkaskoversicherung im Preis enthalten? Ich bin noch nicht so erfahren mit deutschen Autobahnen.","translation":"That sounds good. Is comprehensive insurance included in the price? I'm not very experienced with German autobahns yet.","notes":"question with inverted word order, negation with 'noch nicht so'"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Die Grundversicherung ist dabei, aber für die Vollkasko müssten Sie zusätzlich 12 Euro pro Tag zahlen. Das würde ich Ihnen empfehlen.","translation":"Basic insurance is included, but for comprehensive coverage you'd have to pay an additional 12 euros per day. I would recommend that to you.","notes":"Konjunktiv II 'müssten' and 'würde', separable verb 'dabei sein'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ja, nehmen wir die Vollkasko. Können Sie uns auch eine Karte mit den wichtigsten Autobahnrouten geben?","translation":"Yes, we'll take the comprehensive insurance. Can you also give us a map with the most important autobahn routes?","notes":"imperative form 'nehmen wir', superlative 'wichtigsten'"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Selbstverständlich. Hier ist eine aktuelle Karte. Fahren Sie über die A9 - das ist die direkteste Route nach München.","translation":"Of course. Here's a current map. Drive via the A9 - that's the most direct route to Munich.","notes":"adverb 'selbstverständlich', imperative with 'Sie', superlative 'direkteste'"},{"speaker":"Anna","text":"Entschuldigung, aber gibt es auf deutschen Autobahns wirklich keine Geschwindigkeitsbegrenzung? Das macht mir etwas Angst.","translation":"Excuse me, but is there really no speed limit on German autobahns? That makes me a bit nervous.","notes":"question with 'wirklich', expression 'jemandem Angst machen'"},{"speaker":"Herr Weber","text":"Nicht überall! Viele Abschnitte haben Tempolimits, besonders in der Nähe von Städten. Die Richtgeschwindigkeit liegt bei 130 km/h.","translation":"Not everywhere! Many sections have speed limits, especially near cities. The recommended speed is 130 km/h.","notes":"negation 'nicht überall', temporal adverb 'besonders', preposition 'bei'"},{"speaker":"Max","text":"Ach so! Und was sollten wir sonst noch beachten?
